record.field: NocoDB turns any relational database into a collaborative, spreadsheet-style no-code application platform.

NocoDB turns any relational database into a collaborative, spreadsheet-style no-code application platform.

It connects to MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, SQLite, or MariaDB and exposes the data as smart tables with rich field types, multiple views, and an auto-generated REST API. Teams use it as a self-hosted Airtable alternative to build internal tools, trackers, and lightweight apps without writing backend code, while keeping full ownership of their data. Note: since v0.301.0 it ships under the source-available Sustainable Use License rather than a traditional OSI license.

Key features
  • Grid, Gallery, Form, Kanban, and Calendar views over your tables
  • Rich field types: links, lookups, rollups, formulas, attachments, currency, and more
  • Auto-generated REST API and SDK for every table
  • Webhooks and integrations for chat (Slack, Discord), email (SMTP, AWS SES), and storage (S3, GCS)
  • Role-based access control with granular permissions
  • Works on top of existing databases or its own, fully self-hostable
Best for

Teams and organizations that want an Airtable-like no-code database on their own infrastructure: internal tools, project trackers, CRMs, and data apps where data sovereignty and avoiding per-seat SaaS costs matter.

services: nocodb: image: nocodb/nocodb:latest restart: always depends_on: root_db: condition: service_healthy environment: NC_DB: pg://root_db:5432?u={{ postgres_user }}&p={{ postgres_password }}&d={{ postgres_db }} volumes: - ./nc_data:/usr/app/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network root_db: image: postgres:16.6 restart: always environment: POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" interval: 10s timeout: 5s start_period: 20s retries: 10 volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_root_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T root_db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d root_db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T root_db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T root_db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T root_db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
